content_gaps: The SERP mixes two distinct user intents: those seeking gothic/anatomical human bone replicas (vertebrae, skulls) and those wanting bohemian/tribal hand-carved animal bone (faces, patterns). No top page effectively segments and serves both audiences.

There is a significant lack of detail on ethical sourcing. While terms like 'ethically sourced' are used, no page explains the origin of the bone (e.g., byproduct of the food industry, naturally found) in a way that builds trust.

The cultural and historical symbolism of wearing bone jewelry (memento mori, connection to the natural world, strength) is almost entirely ignored, representing a missed opportunity to connect with buyers on a deeper level.
Detailed information on the craftsmanship is missing. Pages don't discuss the specific techniques for carving bone or the methods of setting the carved pieces in sterling silver.
